domain_score_reviews.reasoning: SAFE instrument adoption establishes €150bn loan facility for joint procurement and Ukraine's association to European Defence Fund represent structural advances in defence integration. However, EDIP funding at 0.2% of total defence spending cannot address immediate capability gaps per ECFR analysis. Insufficient evidence across 2+ sources for upgrade given implementation uncertainties.
key_evidence: SAFE Council adoption confirmed by EEAS (Tier 1)
domain_score_reviews.contradictory_evidence: ECFR notes EDIP €1.5bn represents only 0.2% of European defence spending

domain_score_reviews.reasoning: CADA delay into 2026 and Franco-German digital sovereignty task force signal policy momentum toward cloud/AI sovereignty. However, structural dependency on US hyperscalers and Chinese hardware persists despite 'European effective control' concept development. Single policy development insufficient for score movement.
key_evidence: CADA delay confirmed by Atlantic Council citing MLex (Tier 1)
Franco-German digital sovereignty summit confirmed by Élysée declaration (Tier 1)

domain_score_reviews.reasoning: US State Department withdrawal from counter-FIMI work confirmed by HRVP Kallas forces EU unilateral leadership and weakens G7/NATO coordination. ECFR documents deeper transatlantic misalignment including US Force Posture Review signaling troop reductions. Two Tier 1 sources confirm structural weakening of alliance dependency.
key_evidence: HRVP Kallas keynote: US stopped majority of counter-FIMI work (EEAS Tier 1)
ECFR: European governments reviewing threat assessments toward greater US autonomy (Tier 1)

domain_score_reviews.reasoning: Baltic Sea undersea cable sabotage targets energy infrastructure but no specific energy dependency developments this cycle. Insufficient domain-specific evidence for movement.

Sanctions extension against Russian hybrid actors and crypto financing gap noted, but no direct trade dependency developments. ECFR notes cybersecurity rules blocking Chinese EVs/renewables carry trade implications but remain prospective. Insufficient evidence for movement.

threat_pattern_detections.reasoning: EEAS 4th FIMI Threat Report documents same Russian operational backbone (Doppelganger, African Initiative, Portal Kombat networks) pivoting from Moldova 2025 to Armenia 2026 elections, consistent with patterns across 1st-3rd EEAS reports targeting EU member states.

Drone incursions into Poland/Denmark followed by coordinated disinformation across 50,000 digital signals matches documented Russian kinetic-FIMI hybrid TTP seen in Warsaw arson and Baltic cable sabotage incidents.
Polish PM Tusk confirmed Warsaw shopping centre arson as Russian-ordered, part of physical sabotage pattern including Vilnius and Baltic Sea cables documented across multiple ECFR/EEAS cycles.
Dutch MIVD-reported cyber attacks on public services/critical infrastructure match EEAS-documented Russian hybrid campaign pattern across 3+ reporting cycles.

actor_posture_changes.observation: Sustained hybrid campaign combining cyber attacks, Baltic Sea sabotage, drone incursions (NATO Article 4 triggered), arson attacks, and FIMI pivoting across Moldova/Armenia/EU elections using same infrastructure.
actor_posture_changes.significance: Structural escalation in operational tempo across kinetic, cyber, and information domains erodes EU territory control and democratic integrity simultaneously.

actor_posture_changes.observation: State Department withdrawal from counter-FIMI work slows G7/NATO coordination; Force Posture Review signals further troop reductions.
actor_posture_changes.significance: Structural weakening of transatlantic security architecture forces EU unilateral threat response across information and conventional domains.

cross_monitor_escalation_flags.summary: EEAS 4th FIMI Report identifies Russian infrastructure targeting elections in 8 EU member states (Slovenia, Hungary, Bulgaria, Cyprus, Estonia, Sweden, Latvia, Denmark).
Russian drone incursions into Poland/Denmark airspace triggered NATO Article 4 consultations; Baltic Sea cable sabotage represents kinetic escalation threshold.
Russian FIMI operational backbone with Doppelganger/African Initiative/Portal Kombat networks targeting EU elections and following kinetic incidents with coordinated disinformation.
Crypto financing of Russian sabotage networks evading sanctions enforcement; €150bn SAFE facility carries sovereign debt implications.
AI-generated content and deepfake videos documented in Russian FIMI campaigns; CADA delay affects AI/cloud sovereignty.

analyst_briefing: Domain autonomy scores show targeted movement: diplomatic downgraded from 3→2 due to confirmed US withdrawal from counter-FIMI leadership (HRVP Kallas/EEAS Tier 1) forcing EU unilateral posture amid transatlantic misalignment (ECFR Tier 1). Defence/tech hold at 3/2 despite SAFE €150bn adoption and CADA policy momentum—implementation gaps prevent upgrades. Russian hybrid threat patterns confirmed as structural continuations across FIMI (Moldova→Armenia pivot), kinetic (drone incursions triggering NATO Article 4), sabotage (Warsaw arson), and cyber domains using consistent TTPs documented across 4 EEAS reporting cycles. Russia demonstrates increased operational tempo; US decreased counter-threat engagement. Critical escalations: WDM/SCEM/FCW HIGH urgency for 8 EU elections under FIMI threat and NATO Article 4 triggers. GMM/AGM medium priority for sanctions crypto gaps and AI weaponisation. No contested findings; all Tier 1 sources aligned.
